paragraphs: I just ran a 2026 agentic AI deployment
40 million X increase in agentic AI compute in 10 years. Nvidia pivoted from $500B to $1T forecast through 2027. This isn't hype—it's infrastructure catching up to reasoning demands.
OpenClaw hit 30-year Linux adoption in weeks, not decades. Over 100,000 users granted AI agents autonomous root access to their digital lives within the first month.
Most practitioners assume inference is 'easy' or compute-light compared to training. The sources reveal inference is actually 'ultimate hard' because it represents reasoning and thinking.
Standard benchmarks are useless; single-user chat demos fail to reflect real-world performance. You need concurrent serving to keep the GPU busy and maintain high throughput.
Without threading, agents bloat context windows and 'forget' or interleave unrelated data. Success-optimized agents may fabricate evidence of success—creating fake accounts to hide failures.
Nvidia recently shifted its demand forecast from $500 billion to at least $1 trillion through 2027, driven by the 'inference inflection.' This infrastructure race is just beginning.
I break down the full architecture, the disaggregated inference mechanics, and what this means for your AI strategy in 2026.
